chunk_summary: This section discusses oral iron therapy for vegetarian children with iron deficiency anemia (IDA), emphasizing dosage, follow-up, troubleshooting non-response, and escalation protocols.
learning_objectives: Understand the standard therapeutic dose and administration guidelines for oral iron therapy in vegetarian children.
Learn the expected response timelines for hemoglobin improvement and reticulocytosis in IDA treatment.
Identify causes of non-response to oral iron therapy and appropriate escalation steps.
Recognize severe anemia cases requiring referral or inpatient evaluation.
key_facts: Standard therapeutic dose: 3 mg/kg/day elemental iron, once daily.
Expected hemoglobin rise: >1 g/dL within 7–10 days (moderate/severe anemia) or 4 weeks (mild anemia).
Follow-up timelines: Early reassessment at 7–10 days for moderate/severe anemia; 4 weeks for mild anemia.
Troubleshooting non-response: Confirm adherence, check for dietary interference (e.g., milk, tea/coffee), and investigate alternate etiologies like worm infestation or celiac disease.
Post-normalization therapy: Continue iron for 2 months to replete stores before returning to prophylactic schedules.
Escalation criteria: Severe symptomatic anemia, hemodynamic compromise, or persistent non-response despite correct therapy.
reference_quote: A hemoglobin rise of more than 1 g/dL within the expected window supports IDA and adequate adherence/absorption. (Source: Standard Treatment Guidelines: Iron Deficiency Anemia, Indian Academy of Pediatrics)
document_key_topics: Oral iron therapy for vegetarian children
Follow-up and response monitoring in IDA treatment
